text: Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings Are Ubiquitous in Drugs and Agrochemicals and Have Attracted Increasing Attention in Recent Years as Targets for the Development of Saturated Bioisosteres. While there is great interest in replacing para-Substituted Aromatic Rings with Saturated Bioisosteres, far Less Attention Has Been Paid to Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings. In This Article, We Report the Development of 2-Oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes as Saturated Bioisosteres for Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings. X-Ray Crystal Structures of the Bicyclic Compounds Clearly Show That They Preserve the Stereochemistry of Their Aromatic Analogues. Metabolism Studies Revealed That the Known Fungicides Fluxapyroxad and Boscalid Can Be Modified by Replacing the Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings with the 2-Oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexane Bioisosteres. The Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings can be Replaced by 2-Oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes with a 17-Fold Increase in Water Solubility and a 2.3 Fold Decrease in Log D 7.4. At the Same Time, the Bioactivity of the Fungicides Is Retained. Thus, Ortho-Substituted Phenyl Rings in Bioactive Molecules Can be Replaced with Saturated Bioisosteres as a Means of Increasing the Diversity of Chemical Leads in Medicinal and Agrochemical Research.
